title: Shiv mantra
intro: Read the (Shiv mantra) Om Namah Shivaya Panchakshara Mantra with meaning. Chant this supreme mantra to receive the grace of Lord Shiva.
description:
॥ Om Namah Shivaya ॥

Om Namah Shivaya is called the Panchakshara Mantra as it contains five syllables — Na, Mah, Shi, Va, Ya. It comes from the Shri Rudram of the Yajur Veda and is considered the most beloved mantra of Lord Shiva.

faq.q1: What is the Om Namah Shivaya mantra?
faq.a1: Om Namah Shivaya is the five-syllable (Panchakshara) mantra dedicated to Lord Shiva, meaning 'I bow to Lord Shiva'.
faq.q2: What are the benefits of chanting Om Namah Shivaya?
faq.a2: Chanting it calms the mind, removes negativity, builds self-confidence and bestows the grace of Lord Shiva.
faq.q3: How should Om Namah Shivaya be chanted?
faq.a3: Sit in a quiet place and chant 108 times using a rudraksha mala. Chanting on Mondays, Pradosh and Maha Shivaratri is especially rewarding.
